name: cable rope extension incline bench row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable machine to the low pulley.
Attach a rope handle to the cable machine and sit on the incline bench facing the machine.
Grab the rope handle with an overhand grip and lean forward, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms fully, pulling the rope towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable machine to the low pulley. Attach a rope handle to the cable machine and sit on the incline bench facing the machine. Grab the rope handle with an overhand grip and lean forward, keeping your back straight. Extend your arms fully, pulling the rope towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement. Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Avoid supine positions after the first trimester due to vena cava compression. Incline or side-lie alternatives preferred.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
